extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE attacked with MÜHLBERGER and DE MARCHI in early breakaway, survived slide-out on penultimate descent, won photo finish after MÜHLBERGER led out sprint.
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE targeted KOM jersey by cresting every climb first in break, then took stage win—dual-objective breakaway strategy.
DE MARCHI accelerated hard just before summit knowing flat section prevents counter-attacks, tactical timing over raw power.
MÜHLBERGER refused to take front in final kilometer despite strength, allowing ALAPHILIPPE drafting advantage into sprint.
YATES team had HAIG pacing on steepest sections while KWIATKOWSKI led later, structured domestique rotation on long climb.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: demonstrated exceptional breakaway range by controlling KOM points across all climbs then still winning sprint after 12+ minute gap.
rider_signals.MÜHLBERGER Gregor: positive: matched ALAPHILIPPE on climbs and nearly won sprint, but tactically naive by leading out final 500m.
rider_signals.DE MARCHI Alessandro: positive: timed summit acceleration perfectly to exploit terrain transition, cracked only in final 500m after strong climbing.
rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: defended yellow comfortably with team support but showed no interest attacking despite Jumbo-Visma pacing.
rider_signals.POELS Wout: positive: led peloton sprint from favorites group, unusual role suggests targeting stage time bonuses or testing form.
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: mechanical issue early requiring chain service, lost top-five GC position suggesting cumulative time loss.
